title: Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Evacuation of Ossus}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Ossus}} {{Conjecture}} {{Citation}} {{Battle |prev=[[Cron Supernova/Legends|Cron Supernova]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]] |conc= |next=[[First Battle of Iridonia]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |image=[[File:TSW5fullcoverart.jpg]] |name=Battle of Ossus |conflict=[[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] ([[Krath Holy Crusade]])''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |date=[[3996 BBY]] |place=[[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 5]] |result=*Sith victory *Planet virtually destroyed *Jedi enclave abandoned *[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] leaves the Sith Order. |side1=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side2=[[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Thon]] *[[Nomi Sunrider]] *[[Cay Qel-Droma]]† |commanders2=*[[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] *[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] *Republic soldiers *Republic fleet **''[[Kedriss One]]'' **An [[Evac ship/Legends|evacuation ship]]''[[Nexus of Power]]'' |forces2=*[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] shocktroopers **[[Viper Alpha]] *[[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] soldiers |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*[[Cay Qel-Droma]] *[[Kedriss One]] *An unknown number of Republic soldiers and Jedi Knights. |casual2=*An unknown number of Massassi and Mandalorian soldiers. |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|We have arrived, Exar Kun.''"
"''Yes, Ulic—Ossus is ours for the taking.|Ulic Qel-Droma and Exar Kun|}} A '''battle''' on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] took place in the year [[3996 BBY]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], and was one of the last major battles to be fought during that conflict. With [[Aleema Keto]]'s destruction of the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] through the use of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] magic, an enormous [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] was on a direct course for the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|library]] world of Ossus. With the knowledge that the Sith-created supernova would force the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] to evacuate Ossus, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] led an attack against the Jedi planet, intent on capturing the vast amount of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] knowledge and tomes that had been collected there over the millennia. Armed with a number of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warriors, as well as their army of [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] soldiers, Qel-Droma and Kun stormed Ossus, taking advantage of the confusion and chaos surrounding the devastation headed for the planet. Following their initial invasion, both Sith Lords were involved with various skirmishes, until Ulic was confronted by his brother [[Cay Qel-Droma|Cay]], who was attempting to turn Ulic back to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]]. The two brothers eventually battled, and the resulting fight saw the death of Cay Qel-Droma at the hands of Ulic. [[Nomi Sunrider]], devastated by having witnessed the death of Cay, managed to use her powers to strip Ulic from the Force. Finally realizing how he had allowed the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] to manipulate him, Ulic repented and offered to lead the Jedi to Kun's hidden fortress on the jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Mandalore and most of his warriors have been sent to take the Dark Citadel of Iziz on Onderon. For us, though, Ossus will be easy pickings.|Exar Kun, speaking to Ulic Qel-Droma|}} [[File:JediArtifact.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Jedi artifacts being salvaged from the impending destruction of Ossus.]] During the [[Battle of Kemplex Nine]], a sabotaged ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] device used to manipulate matter within the cores of stars caused [[Cron Supernova/Legends|the detonation]] of the nearby [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] stellar group, triggering a massive supernova shockwave in whose direct path lay the Jedi scholar world of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. A large-scale planetary evacuation was called, with military, civilian, and Jedi personnel alike struggling to escape the planet before the nova-wave arrived. The Jedi Masters planet-side began a rescue operation focused upon salvaging as many relics, manuscripts, and other antiquities as possible from the enclave while there still was time. This would, in part, prove to be too tempting an opportunity for [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] to pass up. Linking up with fellow [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and reinforcements of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] and [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors, and redirecting [[Mandalore the Indomitable|Lord Mandalore the Indomitable's]] main battle group to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], Kun launched a raid upon Ossus itself, intent on capturing the vast quantities of Jedi knowledge stored there. ==The battle== {{Quote|You have corrupted my mate Crado! You have slain Master Vodo! You have shattered the peace of our galaxy!|Sylvar, speaking to Exar Kun|}} [[File:Sylvarmassassi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sylvar engaging Massassi warriors during the battle.]] With the evacuation already reaching critical levels, the Sith and Mandalorian ships converged upon the planet, targeting numerous civilian and military transport craft and engaging the Jedi defenses long enough for Kun to slip beneath the screens and land in the enclave. Among his first targets of choice was the residence of [[Ood Bnar|Master Ood Bnar]], himself attempting to preserve a cache of antique Jedi lightsabers from the oncoming destruction. Engaging Kun and his Massassi, Master Bnar realized that his true skills lay not in combat, but in the powers of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Drawing upon it, the [[Neti/Legends|Neti]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] sealed the cache, metamorphosing into a great tree and thwarting the incredulous [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]]. He would remain planted there until awakened during [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kam Solusar]]'s mission to Ossus in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] Not far afield, the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] Jedi [[Sylvar]] assaulted Kun near his ship, seeking revenge for her mate [[Crado]]'s death at Kemplex Nine, but was beaten by his elite Massassi guard. Meanwhile, [[Cay Qel-Droma]] sensed his brother Ulic's presence amongst the battling orbital and sub-orbital ships, and took the ''[[Nebulon Ranger]]'' to the skies in an effort to sway Ulic back to the light. Shot down by Mandalorian ships during the melée, Cay was forced to crash-land in the city, where the two brothers confronted each other for the very last time. Attempting to reason with him, Cay only succeeded in provoking Ulic's temper—the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] surging within him, Ulic instigated a violent lightsaber duel, shearing off his younger brother's mechanical arm. Pleading with him, Cay tried to reach through to the Jedi deep beneath Ulic…but in one final surge of anger, Ulic slew his brother [[Qel-Droma's Grave|where he knelt]]. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Ulic, you are forever cursed for what you have done. See what has come from your own pride and your appetite for revenge. You brought this upon yourself. The Jedi Masters were right, Ulic…you must face the consequences of what you have begun. Ulic—I ''loved'' you, but this…this! I imprison you in a wall of light. A fortress to block you from the Force…|Nomi Sunrider, to Ulic Qel-Droma following the death of Cay|}} As a final act of punishment and retribution, [[Nomi Sunrider]] imprisoned Ulic beneath a wall of [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] Force-energy. Ulic was forever [[Sever Force/Legends|cut off]] from the Force and captured by the Republic, thereby becoming the last casualty of the battle. The stage would now be set for the final showdown with Exar Kun at Yavin IV. ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Ossus first appeared in the issue [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''The Sith War 5: Brother Against Brother'']] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] series of comics. The battle was conceived and written by author [[Kevin J. Anderson]], and was drawn by artist [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] The cover art for ''Brother Against Brother'', which featured a dueling Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma, was created by [[Hugh Fleming]], a veteran ''Tales of the Jedi'' artist. Anderson used the Battle of Ossus to bring an end to Ulic Qel-Droma's reign as a Sith Lord, and continue the chain of events, begun during the writing of the Battle of Kemplex Nine, which would see the fall of Exar Kun and the end of the Great Sith War. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 5]] {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} {{C|as Destruction of Ossus}} *{{LivingForce|Almas, Sweet
